---
title: "Your Document Title"
author: "Jacopo Olivieri"
date: "2023-02-22"
output:
  html_document:
    fig_width: 6
    fig_aspect_ratio: 0.618
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    echo: false
    warning: false
    message: false
    fig.align: "center"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Initialize packages with version control
if (!requireNamespace("renv", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("renv")
}

# Run manual renv commands if needed
# renv::activate() # Activate the project library
# renv::restore()  # Restore the environment if running for first time
# renv::snapshot()  # After adding/updating packages, snapshot the state


# Load required packages
required_packages <- c("rmarkdown", "rio", "tidyverse", "purrr", "here",
                       "janitor")
install_if_missing <- function(packages) {
  new_packages <- packages[!sapply(packages, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)]
  if (length(new_packages) > 0) {
    install.packages(new_packages)
  }
  invisible(sapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE))
}
install_if_missing(required_packages)


# Initialize logging
log_path <- here::here("output", "log", "explore_data.log")
if (!dir.exists(dirname(log_path))) {
  dir.create(dirname(log_path), recursive = TRUE)
}
logger::log_appender(logger::appender_file(log_path))
logger::log_layout(logger::layout_glue_colors)
```

## Load Data

```{r}
consents_all <- import(here("data", "processed", "consent_discharges_db.RData"))
individ_dat <- import(here("data", "processed", "combined_edm_data.RData"))
annual_dat <- import(here("data", "processed", "annual_return_edm.rds"))
x <- load_data()
```



```{r}

combinations <- expand_grid(
  water_company = unique(annual_dat$water_company),
  year = 2021:2023
)

cols_annual_dat <- c(
      "outlet_discharge_ngr", "wfd_waterbody_id", "wfd_waterbody_name", 
      "shellfish_water", "treatment_type")
cols_annual_dat <- c("outlet_discharge_ngr")

# Process both datasets in one step with map2
merged_data1 <- map2(
  combinations$water_company, 
  combinations$year,
  ~{
    # Get individual data
    indiv <- individ_dat %>%
      filter(water_company == .x, year == .y) %>%
      select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))
    
    # Get annual data
    annual <- annual_dat %>%
      filter(water_company == .x, year == .y, !is.na(counted_spills)) %>%
      select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))
    
    # Get common key columns
    key_join <- intersect(names(indiv), names(annual))
    key_cols <- c(key_join, cols_annual_dat)
    cols_annual_dat <- cols_annual_dat
    
    # Prepare check for many-to-many join
    indiv <- indiv %>% 
      group_by(across(all_of(key_join))) %>%
      mutate(n_individ = n()) %>%
      ungroup() 
    
    # Select key columns (if they exist) and filter out duplicates
    annual <- annual %>%
      select(any_of(key_cols)) %>%
      distinct() %>% 
      group_by(across(all_of(key_join))) %>% 
      mutate(n_annual = n()) %>%
      ungroup()
    
    # Merge and add relationship indicator
    left_join(indiv, annual, by = key_join, suffix = c("_indiv", "_annual")) %>% 
      group_by(across(all_of(key_join))) %>%
      mutate(n_key_join = n()/n_individ) %>%
      ungroup() %>% 
      arrange(across(all_of(key_join))) %>% 
      mutate(key_join = list(key_join)) %>% 
      select(
        any_of(c("n_individ", "n_annual", "n_key_join", 
         "water_company", "year", "site_name_ea", "unique_id", "site_name_wa_sc", 
           "permit_reference_ea", "activity_reference", "permit_reference_wa_sc")),
         everything())
  }
)

resultsy <- map(merged_data2, ~ filter(.x, n_key_join > 1) %>% 
  select(-contains("time")) %>% 
  distinct())

results2 <- map(merged_data2, ~ filter(.x, n_merge > 1) %>% 
  select(-contains("time")) %>% 
  distinct() %>% 
  nrow()) %>% 
  unlist()

resultsx
tibble(x = unlist(flatten(results1)),
       y = unlist(flatten(results2)),
       row = rep(1:length(results1)))



cols_annual_dat <- c(
      "asset_type", "discharge_ngr", "wfd_waterbody_id", "wfd_waterbody_name", 
      "receiving_water_name", "shellfish_water", "edm_commission_date", 
      "edm_operation_percent", "edm_operation_reason", "edm_operation_action", 
      "high_spill_enhancement", "treatment_type", "no_full_years_edm_data")

map(merged_data1, ~ filter(.x, n_key_join > 1) %>% 
  select(-contains("time")) %>% 
    arrange(n_annual, n_key_join))

merged_data1 <- map(merged_data1, ~ filter(.x, !is.na(n_annual)))

counter = 1
for (counter in 1:30) {
  print(counter)
  all.equal(merged_data1[[counter]]$n_annual, merged_data1[[counter]]$n_key_join) %>% 
    print()
}
all.equal(merged_data1[[counter]]$n_annual, merged_data1[[counter]]$n_key_join)
``` 


```{r}
combinations <- expand_grid(
  water_company = unique(annual_dat$water_company),
  year = 2021:2023
)

cols_annual_dat <- c(
      "outlet_discharge_ngr", "wfd_waterbody_id", "wfd_waterbody_name", 
      "shellfish_water", "treatment_type")

cols_annual_dat <- c("outlet_discharge_ngr")

# Process both datasets in one step with map2
merged_data2 <- map2(
  combinations$water_company, 
  combinations$year,
  ~{
    # Get individual data
    indiv <- individ_dat %>%
      filter(water_company == .x, year == .y) %>%
      select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))
    
    # Get annual data
    annual <- annual_dat %>%
      filter(water_company == .x, year == .y, !is.na(counted_spills)) %>%
      select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))
    
    # Get common key columns
    cols_annual_dat <- c("outlet_discharge_ngr")
    key_join <- intersect(names(indiv), names(annual))
    key_cols <- c(key_join, cols_annual_dat)
    
    # Prepare check for many-to-many join
    indiv <- indiv %>% 
      group_by(across(all_of(key_join))) %>%
      mutate(n_individ = n()) %>%
      ungroup() 
    
    # Select joining columns and location column in preparation for merge
    # For some datasets (where each year, water company is a data set)
    # There is no unique identifier for each location
    # We therefore use the unique location which has the highest spill duration
    # We mark rows without a unique identifier by n_annual > 1
    annual <- annual %>%
      distinct(across(all_of(key_cols)), total_duration_all_spills_hrs) %>% 
      group_by(across(all_of(key_cols))) %>%
      mutate(
        max_duration_all_spills_hrs = max(total_duration_all_spills_hrs),
        .keep = "unused") %>% 
      distinct(across(all_of(key_cols)), max_duration_all_spills_hrs) %>% 
      group_by(across(all_of(key_join))) %>%
      mutate(n_annual = n()) %>%
      slice(which.max(max_duration_all_spills_hrs)) %>% 
      select(all_of(key_cols), n_annual) %>% 
      ungroup() %>% 
      arrange(desc(n_annual))
    
    # Merge and add relationship indicator
    left_join(indiv, annual, by = key_join, suffix = c("_indiv", "_annual")) %>% 
      arrange(across(all_of(key_join))) %>% 
      mutate(key_join = list(key_join)) %>% 
      select(
        any_of(c("n_individ", "n_annual", "n_key_join", 
         "water_company", "year", "site_name_ea", "unique_id", "site_name_wa_sc", 
           "permit_reference_ea", "activity_reference", "permit_reference_wa_sc")),
         everything())
  }
)

```

```{r}
combinations <- expand_grid(
  water_company = unique(annual_dat$water_company),
  year = 2021:2023
)

# Process both datasets in one step with map2
merged_data2 <- map2(
  combinations$water_company, 
  combinations$year,
  ~{
    # Get individual data
    indiv <- individ_dat %>%
      filter(water_company == .x, year == .y) %>%
      select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))
    
    # Get annual data
    annual <- annual_dat %>%
      filter(water_company == .x, year == .y, !is.na(counted_spills)) %>%
      select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))
    
    # Get common key columns
    cols_annual_dat <- c("outlet_discharge_ngr")
    key_join <- intersect(names(indiv), names(annual))
    key_cols <- c(key_join, cols_annual_dat)
    
    # Prepare check for many-to-many join
    indiv <- indiv %>% 
      group_by(across(all_of(key_join))) %>%
      mutate(n_individ = n()) %>%
      ungroup() 
    
    # Select joining columns and location column in preparation for merge
    # For some datasets (where each year, water company is a data set)
    # There is no unique identifier for each location
    # We therefore use the unique location which has the highest spill duration
    # We mark rows without a unique identifier by n_annual > 1
    annual <- annual %>%
      distinct(across(all_of(key_cols)), total_duration_all_spills_hrs) %>% 
      group_by(across(all_of(key_cols))) %>%
      mutate(
        max_duration_all_spills_hrs = max(total_duration_all_spills_hrs),
        .keep = "unused") %>% 
      distinct(across(all_of(key_cols)), max_duration_all_spills_hrs) %>% 
      group_by(across(all_of(key_join))) %>%
      mutate(n_annual = n()) %>%
      slice(which.max(max_duration_all_spills_hrs)) %>% 
      select(all_of(key_cols), n_annual) %>% 
      ungroup() %>% 
      arrange(desc(n_annual))
    
    # Merge and add relationship indicator
    left_join(indiv, annual, by = key_join, suffix = c("_indiv", "_annual")) %>% 
      arrange(across(all_of(key_join))) %>% 
      mutate(key_join = list(key_join)) %>% 
      select(
        any_of(c("n_individ", "n_annual", "n_key_join", 
         "water_company", "year", "site_name_ea", "unique_id", "site_name_wa_sc", 
           "permit_reference_ea", "activity_reference", "permit_reference_wa_sc")),
         everything())
  }
)

x2 <- map(merged_data2, ~ filter(.x, n_annual > 1) %>% nrow()) %>% unlist()

x2 <- map(merged_data2, ~ filter(.x, is.na(n_annual)) %>% nrow()) %>% unlist()

```


```{r}
# Create all possible combinations of water companies and years
combinations <- expand_grid(
 water_company = unique(annual_dat$water_company),
 year = 2021:2023
)

# Process datasets for each company-year combination
merged_data <- map2(
 combinations$water_company, 
 combinations$year,
 ~{
   # Extract current company and year for readability
   current_company <- .x
   current_year <- .y
   
   # Get individual and annual spill data for current company-year
   # Remove columns that are all NA as they may differ between datasets
   indiv <- individ_dat %>%
     filter(water_company == current_company, year == current_year) %>%
     select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))
   annual <- annual_dat %>%
     filter(water_company == current_company, year == current_year, !is.na(counted_spills)) %>%
     select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))
   
   # Define joining keys: common columns plus location identifier
   cols_annual_dat <- c("outlet_discharge_ngr")
   key_join <- intersect(names(indiv), names(annual))
   key_cols <- c(key_join, cols_annual_dat)
   
   # Count occurrences of each key combination in individual data
   indiv <- indiv %>% 
     group_by(across(all_of(key_join))) %>%
     mutate(n_individ = n()) %>%
     ungroup() 
   
   # Process annual data to handle non-unique location matches:
   annual <- annual %>%
     # Get unique combinations of keys and spill durations
     distinct(across(all_of(key_cols)), total_duration_all_spills_hrs) %>% 
     # For each key combination, identify the maximum spill duration
     group_by(across(all_of(key_cols))) %>%
     mutate(
       max_duration_all_spills_hrs = max(total_duration_all_spills_hrs),
       used_max_duration = TRUE,
       .keep = "unused"
     ) %>% 
     # Remove duplicate max duration values
     distinct(across(all_of(key_cols)), max_duration_all_spills_hrs) %>% 
     # Count annual records per key to identify non-unique matches
     group_by(across(all_of(key_join))) %>%
     mutate(
       n_annual = n(),
       used_max_duration = n_annual > 1  # TRUE if multiple locations for this key
     ) %>%
     # Keep only the location with maximum duration
     slice(which.max(max_duration_all_spills_hrs)) %>% 
     select(all_of(key_cols), n_annual, used_max_duration) %>% 
     ungroup() 
   
   # Merge datasets and add quality indicators
   left_join(indiv, annual, by = key_join, suffix = c("_indiv", "_annual")) %>% 
     arrange(across(all_of(key_join))) %>% 
     mutate(
       # Store joining keys for reference
       key_join = list(key_join),
       # Classify quality of each match:
       # - perfect_match: unique match in both datasets
       # - used_max_duration: location chosen based on maximum duration
       # - multiple_matches: other many-to-many relationships
       merge_quality = case_when(
         n_annual == 1 ~ "perfect_match",
         is.na(n_annual) ~ "no_match",  
         used_max_duration ~ "used_max_duration",
         TRUE ~ "multiple_matches"
       )
     ) %>% 
     # Organize columns with metadata first, then remaining data
     select(
       any_of(c(
         "merge_quality", "n_individ", "n_annual", "used_max_duration",
         "water_company", "year", "site_name_ea", "unique_id", 
         "site_name_wa_sc", "permit_reference_ea", "activity_reference", 
         "permit_reference_wa_sc"
       )),
       everything()
     )
 }
)

x1 <- map(merged_data, ~ filter(.x, n_annual > 1) %>% nrow()) %>% unlist()

p1 <- map(merged_data, 
    ~ .x %>% 
      group_by(water_company, year, merge_quality) %>% 
      summarise(n_merge_dataset = n(), .groups = "drop") %>% 
      mutate(frac_merge_dataset = n_merge_dataset / sum(n_merge_dataset))) %>% 
  bind_rows() 

p2 <- p1 %>% 
  group_by(merge_quality) %>% 
  summarise(n_merge_all = sum(n_merge_dataset), .groups = "drop") %>% 
  mutate(frac_merge_all = n_merge_all / sum(n_merge_all))

```

```{r}
# Create comprehensive merge quality summary
merge_summary <- merged_data %>%
 map_dfr(
   ~.x %>%
     group_by(water_company, year, merge_quality) %>%
     summarise(
       n_records = n(),
       n_locations = n_distinct(outlet_discharge_ngr),
       .groups = "drop"
     ) %>%
     group_by(water_company, year) %>%
     mutate(
       frac_records = n_records / sum(n_records),
       frac_locations = n_locations / sum(n_locations)
     ) %>%
     ungroup(),
   .id = "dataset_index"
 )

# Create summary statistics at different levels
summary_stats <- list(
 # Overall summary
 overall = merge_summary %>%
   group_by(merge_quality) %>%
   summarise(
     total_records = sum(n_records),
     total_locations = sum(n_locations),
     n_datasets = n_distinct(dataset_index),
     .groups = "drop"
   ) %>%
   mutate(
     frac_records = total_records / sum(total_records),
     frac_locations = total_locations / sum(total_locations),
     perc_records = scales::percent(frac_records, accuracy = 0.1),
     perc_locations = scales::percent(frac_locations, accuracy = 0.1)
   ),
 
 # By company summary
 by_company = merge_summary %>%
   group_by(water_company, merge_quality) %>%
   summarise(
     total_records = sum(n_records),
     total_locations = sum(n_locations),
     n_years = n_distinct(year),
     .groups = "drop"
   ) %>%
   group_by(water_company) %>%
   mutate(
     frac_records = total_records / sum(total_records),
     frac_locations = total_locations / sum(total_locations),
     perc_records = scales::percent(frac_records, accuracy = 0.1),
     perc_locations = scales::percent(frac_locations, accuracy = 0.1)
   ),
 
 # By year summary
 by_year = merge_summary %>%
   group_by(year, merge_quality) %>%
   summarise(
     total_records = sum(n_records),
     total_locations = sum(n_locations),
     n_companies = n_distinct(water_company),
     .groups = "drop"
   ) %>%
   group_by(year) %>%
   mutate(
     frac_records = total_records / sum(total_records),
     frac_locations = total_locations / sum(total_locations),
     perc_records = scales::percent(frac_records, accuracy = 0.1),
     perc_locations = scales::percent(frac_locations, accuracy = 0.1)
   ),
   
 # By company-year combination
 by_company_year = merge_summary %>%
   group_by(water_company, year, merge_quality) %>%
   summarise(
     total_records = sum(n_records),
     total_locations = sum(n_locations),
     .groups = "drop"
   ) %>%
   group_by(water_company, year) %>%
   mutate(
     frac_records = total_records / sum(total_records),
     frac_locations = total_locations / sum(total_locations),
     perc_records = scales::percent(frac_records, accuracy = 0.1),
     perc_locations = scales::percent(frac_locations, accuracy = 0.1)
   )
)

# Create ordered output
formatted_summary <- list(
 overall_stats = summary_stats$overall %>%
   arrange(desc(total_records)),
 
 by_company = summary_stats$by_company %>%
   arrange(water_company, desc(total_records)),
 
 by_year = summary_stats$by_year %>%
   arrange(year, desc(total_records)),
   
 by_company_year = summary_stats$by_company_year %>%
   arrange(water_company, year, desc(total_records))
)

```



```{r}
data_list
merge_location(data_list$individual, data_list$annual) %>% 
  summarise_merge()

process_data(data_list$individual, data_list$annual, summarise = TRUE)
```
